title: FundedNext 15% OFF
description: FundedNext, based in the United Arab Emirates, was established in March 2022. The firm is a CFD provider offering 1 Step and 2 Steps program types. Account sizes range from $5,000 to $200,000. Platforms supported include cTrader, MT4 and MT5. Tradable instruments include Forex, Commodities and Indices, with leverage options up to 1:100 for majors, exotics, and cryptocurrencies. FundedNext has a 4.6/5 review score based on 14,350 reviews. Verified by our [Intelligence Hub](/) for prop trading excellence.

firm_rules.copy_trading: Copy trading is only allowed between challenge accounts and between instant accounts.
firm_rules.expert_advisor: EAs are only available as an add-on. EAs are allowed, only works on MetaTrader 4 and MetaTrader 5. EA must be customized to suit your trading style. Identical trades across accounts are not allowed. Switching between EA and manual trading is not allowed. There is a maximum allocation limit of $300,000 for each EA or trading bot's strategy.
firm_rules.inactivity_rule: To avoid the account inactivity period, traders can simply login to their account at least once or execute a single trade within a period of 30 consecutive days.
firm_rules.news_trading: Allowed without restriction during evaluation stage. Allowed with restriction on funded stage and instant accounts (Trades executed 5 minutes before and after a High Impact News event are subject to a 40% news profit split rule.)
firm_rules.weekend_holding: Allowed (News trading allowed with 40% split rule on funded/instant).

seo_content:
FundedNext: UAE-Based CFD Prop Firm Since 2022

FundedNext is a CFD prop trading firm based in the United Arab Emirates, established in March 2022. The firm has grown rapidly and holds a 4.6 out of 5 review score based on 14,350 trader reviews, making it one of the most positively reviewed firms in the sector. FundedNext offers both 1-step (Stellar) and 2-step (Stellar) evaluation programs across a wide range of account sizes.

The Stellar Challenge lineup spans account sizes from $6,000 to $200,000 in both 1-step and 2-step formats. Sample pricing with the current 15% discount: 200K at $1,022.99 (standard $1,099.99), 100K Stellar 1-Step at $530.09 (standard $569.99), 50K at $306.89, 25K at $204.59, 15K at $120.89, and 6K at $61.37. The 2-step 100K is available at $511.49.

Supported platforms include cTrader, Match Trader, MT4, and MT5. Tradable instruments cover Forex, Metals, Indices, Energy, and Crypto. Leverage varies by instrument and program: Forex goes up to 1:100 on the 2-step program and 1:30 on 1-step; Metals offer 1:10 to 1:15; Indices 1:5 to 1:15; Crypto is capped at 1:1. EAs are available as an add-on and work only on MT4/MT5, with a maximum allocation limit of $300,000 per EA strategy.

Payout frequency is competitive: the Stellar 1-Step program pays every 7 days, while the 2-Step program has a 21-day first payout then 14-day cycles thereafter. The minimum withdrawal is $20. On funded accounts and instant accounts, trades executed within 5 minutes before or after a high-impact news event are subject to a 40% news profit split rule. Payout methods include Crypto (USDT TRC20, USDT ERC20, USDC ERC20), Riseworks, and TC Pay.

FundedNext appeals to traders who want flexible evaluation models, rapid payout cycles on 1-step accounts, and a broad instrument range including crypto and energy. The firm supports a wide payment ecosystem including Apple Pay, Google Pay, PayPal, Skrill, Astropay, and Perfect Money. Weekend holding is permitted, and the firm maintains copy trading restrictions to own-account pairs only, with a $300,000 maximum per EA strategy.
